pages.title: About this decision site
pages.description: Why this site exists and what problem it is trying to solve for Florida homeowners after the report arrives.
pages.quickAnswer: This site exists for the gap between the report arriving and the homeowner choosing the safest next project and contractor path, with a transparent virtual editorial team keeping the route logic narrow.
pages.intro: This site is built for the moment after the inspection report arrives and the homeowner still has to decide what to do next without mistaking editorial guidance for official approval.
whatNotToAssume: Do not assume this site is the program.
Do not assume the goal is broad eligibility traffic.
Do not assume contractor routing comes before recommendation clarity.
bodySections: The goal is to explain what a recommendation likely means, what the current program rules appear to support, and which contractor type to vet first.
The site is not a contractor directory pretending to be editorial. It is a decision layer between the report and a real quote decision.
The first launch surface stays narrow on inspection-report interpretation, project choice, quote preparation, and the main eligible improvement categories.
A transparent virtual editorial team checks rule freshness, route clarity, and homeowner risk. It is an internal QA framework, not a licensed review board.

pages.title: Methodology
pages.description: How the site handles current rules, source freshness, and route promotion discipline.
pages.quickAnswer: Current official pages and support-center articles win, and the virtual editorial team uses rule freshness, route clarity, and homeowner risk lanes to block sloppy promotion.
pages.intro: The site reads current official pages and support-center articles first, then uses older PDFs only for background and runs each route through a transparent virtual editorial checklist.
whatNotToAssume: Do not assume old PDFs outrank current support articles.
Do not assume a held route should auto-promote because it exists.
Do not assume stale source support is safe enough for expansion.

primaryCta.label: See what the program may pay for

secondaryCta.label: Go back to the report decision path

bodySections: Program rules are separated from contractor routing so the site does not imply approval certainty or official control over contractor outcomes.
Each route keeps a source stack, a verified date, and a next review date so stale source support can block promotion.
The virtual editorial team has three internal lanes: Rule Freshness, Route Clarity, and Homeowner Risk. These are internal QA checks, not licensed reviewers.
Support routes stay noindex until there is evidence that they add real decision value beyond the Support Center.

pages.title: Contact
pages.description: How to reach the site operator without implying program affiliation.
pages.quickAnswer: Use this contact path for editorial corrections or a paid pilot conversation tied to one improvement route, not for official program case handling.
pages.intro: Use this contact page for site feedback, factual corrections, or a paid pilot inquiry tied to one improvement-specific route.
whatNotToAssume: Do not assume this inbox can resolve an official application issue.
Do not assume partner outreach belongs on broad generic pages.
Do not assume a route should stay live if its source stack changed.

primaryCta.label: Return to the contractor quote route

bodySections: This inbox is for the editorial product, not for official program case management.
If a page appears stale or unclear, send the route path and the source that changed so the next review can happen quickly.
Licensed Florida contractors can use this path for a paid pilot conversation when the fit stays narrow around one route, one scope discipline, and clear county coverage.
Commercial conversations should stay tied to improvement-specific routes instead of broad directory placement.

pages.title: Not government affiliated
pages.description: A plain-language disclosure that this site is not the program and cannot guarantee approval or reimbursement.
pages.quickAnswer: This is an independent editorial and routing site, not the program, the State of Florida, or an approval channel.
pages.intro: This site is an independent editorial and routing product. It is not the My Safe Florida Home program, the State of Florida, or an official case-management channel.
whatNotToAssume: Do not assume any page here can approve a grant.
Do not assume any route guarantees reimbursement.
Do not assume contractor language on this site overrides official rules.

primaryCta.label: Read what to do after the report

secondaryCta.label: See how the site verifies rules

bodySections: The site does not approve grants, assign inspections, or decide reimbursement.
Any contractor or checklist guidance here should still be verified against your report and the current official program rules.
No page should be read as a promise of approval, reimbursement, or savings.

pages.title: Privacy
pages.description: Basic privacy terms for lead capture and event logging.
pages.quickAnswer: The site stores limited form and event data to understand which routes and recommendation types produce useful next-action signals.
pages.intro: The site stores the lead form fields and event data needed to understand which routes and recommendation types create useful next-action signals.
whatNotToAssume: Do not assume phone is required.
Do not assume the form is the right place for official account credentials.
Do not assume stored route data is the same thing as official program data.
bodySections: The phase-1 build is email-first and does not require a phone number.
Operational storage is file-backed in the current implementation.
Do not submit official account credentials or sensitive program documents through this form.

pages.title: Terms
pages.description: Basic usage terms for the phase-1 editorial product.
pages.quickAnswer: Use the site as a decision aid built on current public information, not as legal, insurance, permitting, or official program advice.
pages.intro: The site provides scenario guidance based on current public information and does not replace legal, insurance, permitting, or official program advice.
whatNotToAssume: Do not assume route copy is a legal guarantee.
Do not assume a recommendation page replaces permit or insurance review.
Do not assume commercial routing should happen before the recommendation is clear.
bodySections: Use the site as a decision aid, not as a guarantee of eligibility or reimbursement.
Project choices should still be verified against your report, permit requirements, and the current official program materials.
Commercial routing should happen only after the homeowner understands the recommendation and likely scope.
